rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALBION NIXON TRUST (*****COTTAGE GROVE REVOCABLE TRUST TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO*****)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: The motion to amend is granted, no opposition filed. Any party requesting to argue the tentative must call the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on 12/14/11.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARGUERITA L. RUBEL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner D.R. Rubel'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulation Between Parties And For Injuctive Relief; P & A In Support; Declaration Of B.F. Connors In Support.
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner Daniel Rubel's Motion to Enforce Stipulation Between Parties and for Injunctive Relief is granted in part and denied in part. Respondent Donald Crabtree shall produce a copy of the escrow instructions, closing papers from the sale of 393 Marina Blvd., San Francisco, and verification of the deposit of the net proceeds of sale of the Marina Blvd. property to Daniel Rubel within five days. Petitioner's request that any net proceeds from the sale of the house which have already been spent be returned is denied. Donald Crabtree shall hold and maintain all remaining net proceeds until resolution of the contest of the Third and Fourth Amendments is decided. The request for an injunction is denied. The request for an evidentiary hearing is denied. There are no disputed issues of fact. The dispute is regarding the language of the stipulation. The interpretation of the stipulation is a question of law for the court. Any party who wishes to argue the matter must notify opposing counsel and the court at 415-551-3702 by 5:00 p.m. on 12/14/11.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIS HANNA WALT TRUST
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. Must Respondents serve a consumer notice on a personal representative of a deceased person in order to obtain discovery of that person¿s individual bank records? The motion to quash is denied as to the records of the trusts and of Petitioner, except that records for the trusts need only be produced beginning as of 2004, and records of Petitioner as of 2010. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend either (a) to exclude claims based on Willis Hannawalt¿s alleged wrongdoing because such claims are subject to the statutory creditor claim procedures or (b) to allege compliance with such creditor¿s claim requirements. Any party requesting to argue the tentative must call the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on 12/14/11.
